_comment: Mutation-testing gate for the money-critical commerce paths. Scoped to the fee/escrow/payment DECISION lines (the notification-copy tails of shipListing/confirmListingDelivery/releaseExpiredReservation are deliberately OUT of range — copy, not money logic) so the score measures money-test strength. Ranges: completeListingPurchase (fee math, ship-by deadline, reserved ledger event), purchaseListing (fee math), shipListing capture-vs-dead-auth + captured ledger event, confirmListingDelivery capture + released ledger event, releaseExpiredReservation cancel/revert + cancelled ledger event, payCommission+releaseCommissionFunds+refundCommissionPayment+finalizeCommissionPayment + captured ledger event. The payment_events ledger args (event_type, amount_nok, fee_nok) ARE in range and asserted by the money suites (db.find('payment_events',...)). Run: npm run test:mutation. break=92 enforces the high-signal floor; residual survivors are fail()/label copy strings, the un-simulatable single-threaded race guard (if(!reverted.length)...), and equivalent initializers (let onboarded=false) — deliberately not chased (would need brittle copy assertions or concurrency).
